title: Verisim Synthesized-Edge Feedback
description: Contract-mediated payload of candidate synthesized call edges that Verisim's runtime traces recovered against OpenLore's static call graph. Additive and provenance-labeled only: every edge is confidence='synthesized', synthesizedBy='verisim-runtime', and evidence-bearing. OpenLore (or a local validator stand-in) ingests this on its own terms; Verisim never writes OpenLore's database. Schema version 1.

fixtureSourceSha.description: The fixture source revision the traces pertain to, for attribution to a known source state.

generatedAgainst.description: Pins the payload to the exact static graph state it was diffed against, so an ingest can detect a payload computed against a different graph.

edges.description: Candidate synthesized edges. Each conforms to OpenLore's CallEdge shape plus an evidence block.

callerId.description: OpenLore node id of the call site (filepath::name).

calleeId.description: OpenLore node id of the invoked node.

line.description: Always null: a runtime exec has no source-level call-site line.

callType.description: Always null: no AST call shape behind a subprocess invocation.

evidence.description: Why the edge is claimed: the runtime trace that witnessed the invocation.

findings.description: Secondary architectural-invariant findings (reserved; empty in the prototype).

dropped.description: Count of runtime invocations that could not be anchored to a known node (precision over recall; surfaced, not silently discarded).
